    Home › Airlines › Qantas

    QF Qantas

    Australia · oneworld · Hub: Sydney (SYD)

    The Australian flag carrier — a century old, flying the A380 on the Kangaroo Route and trans-Pacific trunk lines.

    Types in fleet3
    Biggest aircraftAirbus A380
    Allianceoneworld
    Founded1920
    IATAQF
    Side by side

    The fleet at true scale

    Every type Qantas flies, drawn to the same scale — widest wings on the left. Tap any silhouette.

    Airbus A380
    Airbus A330
    Boeing 737
    The fleet

    What Qantas flies

    Grid
    10 in fleet

    Airbus A380

    Airbus · Out of production (2021) — in service

    The full fleet returned by December 2025 after the largest maintenance program in the airline's history.

    Full specification →
    ≈25 in fleet

    Airbus A330

    Airbus · In production (A330neo)

    Full specification →
    ≈75 in fleet

    Boeing 737

    Boeing · In production (MAX series)

    The 737-800 is the backbone of the domestic network.

    Full specification →
    On order

    Airbus A350

    Airbus · In production

    A350-1000ULRs on order for Project Sunrise — nonstop Sydney to London and New York.

    Full specification →
    Flies Airbus A380 — the largest passenger aircraft ever built →
    Route records

    Their longest flights

    RouteAircraftDistanceBlock time
    Sydney (SYD) → London Heathrow (LHR) announcedAirbus A350-1000ULR17,015 km~22 h
    Sydney (SYD) → New York JFK (JFK) announcedAirbus A350-1000ULR16,020 km~21 h
    Perth (PER) → London Heathrow (LHR)Boeing 787-914,499 km17 h 20
    Dallas/Fort Worth (DFW) → Melbourne (MEL)Boeing 787-914,468 km17 h 35
    Dallas/Fort Worth (DFW) → Sydney (SYD)Airbus A38013,804 km17 h 05

    From our longest flights in the world board.

    Good to know

    Qantas, in a few facts

    • Has never lost a jet airliner in a fatal accident — a safety record made famous by Rain Man.
    • Project Sunrise: from 2027 it plans to fly Sydney–London nonstop, the longest flight ever scheduled.
    Sources
    • Qantas — our aircraft ↗

    About the data. Every specification is compiled from manufacturer documents and cited per row. Operator fleet counts are approximate snapshots, marked ≈ and dated; individually verified figures carry a ✓. All silhouettes are original works of aircraft.fyi, drawn to true scale. Corrections and enquiries: business@luck.fyi — we fix errors visibly, not silently.
